name: Cloudflare Pages
description: Edge-first static site and full-stack hosting platform built on Cloudflare's global network with unlimited bandwidth and generous free tier

description.value: A JAMstack and full-stack hosting platform that deploys static assets and serverless functions to Cloudflare's global edge network, offering unlimited bandwidth on all plans, built-in DDoS protection, and deep integration with Cloudflare's developer platform including Workers, KV, D1, and R2.

year-launched.comment: Beta announced December 2020, General Availability in April 2021

open-source.comment: The Pages platform itself is proprietary, though the Workers runtime (workerd) is open source

primary-focus.comment: Originally focused on JAMstack static sites, now strongly edge-first with Workers integration and full-stack framework support

free-tier-bandwidth.comment: Cloudflare does not charge for bandwidth on any plan, including the free tier

free-tier-build-minutes.comment: Measured in number of builds rather than build minutes; 1 concurrent build on free plan; builds timeout after 20 minutes each

free-tier-sites.comment: Unlimited sites with a soft limit of 100 projects per account to prevent abuse; can be raised by contacting support

paid-plan-starting-price.comment: Workers Paid plan at $5/month includes 20,000 builds/month and 5 concurrent builds for Pages; separate from the $20/month Cloudflare Pro plan for other features

bandwidth-overage-cost.comment: Cloudflare does not charge for bandwidth or data transfer on any plan

enterprise-pricing.comment: Cloudflare offers Enterprise plans with custom pricing, dedicated support, and SLAs

git-integration.comment: Native integration with GitHub and GitLab; Bitbucket and other providers supported via Direct Upload with Wrangler CLI in CI/CD pipelines

auto-deploy.comment: Pushes to connected branches automatically trigger deployments; can be disabled via branch deployment controls

cli-deployment.comment: Wrangler CLI supports Direct Upload of pre-built assets via 'wrangler pages deploy'

build-system.comment: Built-in CI/CD build pipeline with configurable build commands, root directory, and environment variables

supported-frameworks.comment: Extensive framework support including Next.js (via @opennextjs/cloudflare), Nuxt, Gatsby, Astro, Hugo, Jekyll, SvelteKit, Remix, Angular, Vue, Eleventy, Vite, plus Qwik, SolidStart, Docusaurus, and more

monorepo-support.comment: Supports configurable root directory and build watch paths; works with Turborepo, Nx, and Lerna

deploy-previews.comment: Every commit gets a unique hash-based preview URL; branch aliases auto-update to latest commit (e.g., branch-name.project.pages.dev)

instant-rollbacks.comment: Any previous successful production deployment can be promoted instantly without rebuilding; available via dashboard and API

deploy-notifications.comment: Email notifications via Cloudflare Notifications; webhooks for external services (Slack, Google Chat, etc.); GitHub commit status checks on PRs; PagerDuty available on Business+ plans

global-cdn.comment: All static assets served from Cloudflare's global anycast network

cdn-provider.comment: Uses Cloudflare's own global anycast network, not a third-party CDN

edge-locations.comment: One of the largest edge networks globally; handles approximately 20% of all web traffic

http2.comment: HTTP/2 enabled by default with multiplexing and server push

http3.comment: HTTP/3 with QUIC protocol supported for reduced latency

response-headers.comment: Custom response headers configurable via _headers file in the project output directory

image-optimization.comment: Cloudflare Images and Polish available as add-ons; Polish compresses images automatically; 5,000 free image transformations/month included with all accounts; not built directly into Pages but available as a Cloudflare platform feature

asset-compression.comment: Automatic content compression; Brotli available on Pro+ plans, Gzip and Zstandard on all plans

custom-domains.comment: Custom domains and subdomains supported; DNS records and SSL certificates provisioned automatically

free-ssl.comment: SSL certificates automatically provisioned for custom domains at no cost

wildcard-ssl.comment: Wildcard custom domains are not supported on Cloudflare Pages; wildcard SSL with proxied records is an Enterprise feature on the broader Cloudflare platform

auto-https-redirect.comment: Always Use HTTPS setting automatically redirects all HTTP requests to HTTPS

custom-domain-preview.comment: Custom domains cannot be directly attached to preview deployments; only the default hash-based and branch-alias *.pages.dev URLs are provided. Workarounds exist using Workers as a proxy.

serverless-functions.comment: Pages Functions provide file-system based routing for serverless functions that run on Cloudflare Workers

functions-runtime.comment: Runs on the workerd runtime (V8 isolates); first-class support for JavaScript/TypeScript (Node.js compatible APIs), Python (via Pyodide/WASM, in beta), and Rust/C/C++/Go via WebAssembly. Not traditional Node.js but provides Node.js-compatible APIs.

edge-functions.comment: All Pages Functions run at the edge by default on Cloudflare's global network; this is a core architectural advantage

scheduled-functions.comment: Cron Triggers are a Cloudflare Workers feature and are not directly supported in Pages Functions; requires a separate Worker for scheduled tasks

form-handling.comment: No built-in form handling like Netlify Forms; can be implemented manually via Pages Functions or third-party services

authentication.comment: Cloudflare Access (part of Zero Trust) provides authentication and access control; free for up to 50 users; not built into Pages directly but integrates seamlessly

kv-storage.comment: Workers KV is available as a binding in Pages Functions; globally distributed key-value storage with free tier

database-integration.comment: D1 (serverless SQLite database), R2 (object storage), Durable Objects (stateful edge compute), and Hyperdrive (connection pooling for external Postgres/MySQL) all available as bindings

redirects.comment: Configurable via _redirects file; supports wildcards, splats, placeholders, query strings, and fragments; 301 and 302 status codes

rewrites.comment: Proxying with 200 status code supported for relative URLs within the same site via _redirects file; external domain proxying not supported natively but achievable via Pages Functions or Workers

custom-404.comment: A 404.html file in the output directory serves as the custom not-found page; note that having 404.html disables automatic SPA fallback routing

spa-fallback.comment: If no 404.html is present, Pages automatically serves the root index.html for all unmatched paths, enabling SPA client-side routing

headers-config.comment: Custom response headers configurable via _headers file with per-path rules; applies to static assets only (not Pages Functions responses)

ab-testing.comment: A/B testing implementable via Pages Functions middleware with cookie-based traffic splitting; not a built-in toggle but well-documented with official examples

free-team-members.comment: No practical limit on team members for Cloudflare accounts on any plan including free

rbac.comment: Role-based access controls available on all plans including free; multiple roles can be assigned per user

audit-logs.comment: Audit logs available on all plan types, free of charge, always enabled; searchable by user email and domain with date range filtering

environment-variables.comment: Environment variables and encrypted secrets supported per environment (production/preview); configurable via dashboard or Wrangler config

preview-comments.comment: No built-in preview commenting feature like Vercel or Netlify; this has been requested by the community but is not yet available

ddos-protection.comment: Unmetered and unlimited DDoS protection at layers 3, 4, and 7 included on all plans; autonomous detection and mitigation, typically within 3 seconds

waf.comment: Cloudflare WAF available; managed rulesets included on Pro+ plans; free plan includes basic security rules

soc2.comment: Cloudflare maintains SOC 2 Type II certification covering security, confidentiality, and availability trust service criteria

gdpr.comment: GDPR compliant; ISO 27701 certified as both data processor and controller; Data Processing Addendum available

password-protection.comment: Not a native one-click feature; achievable via Cloudflare Access (free for up to 50 users) or via Pages Functions implementing HTTP Basic Auth; requires additional configuration

ip-access-control.comment: IP-based access control available via Cloudflare Access policies; can restrict access by IP address, email, or SSO identity

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive developer documentation with framework guides, API references, tutorials, and examples; well-organized and regularly updated; minor gaps around some newer integrations

integrations.comment: Analytics via Cloudflare Web Analytics (free); CMS partnerships with Sanity, Strapi, Contentful, WordPress; monitoring via Logpush to Datadog, Splunk, etc.; auth via Cloudflare Access; database via D1, Hyperdrive, and bindings

community.comment: Active Discord server (85,000+ members), Cloudflare Community forum, Stack Overflow presence; no GitHub Discussions for Pages specifically

status-page.comment: Public status page at cloudflarestatus.com with incident history, scheduled maintenance, and subscription options
